ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-02-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ernest Der for and with Debra Dolch.

The Court expressed concern about the amount of fees being sought. Mr. Der stated that his Client has to balance what is in the Trust and the needs of her Client. Ms. Dolch stated that fees went up significantly in the last accounting period because of the relationship Philip had with his Case Manager, that Philip was very close with the Case Manager and that the Case Manager spent a lot of time with Philip. Ms. Dolch further explained that the Care Manager retired about a year and a half ago, that the new one does not spend a lot time with Philip, that Philip spends a lot of time in the street, that she is having to buy him clothes because he gives his away, that the relationship with the new Case Manager is getting better, that she will spend less time with him and that she is willing to defer her fees. Mr. Der stated that the Trust was about $120,000.00 when they started out. The Sixth Account and Report, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-29-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for and with (trustee) Debra Dolch.

Mr. Der addressed the concern regarding the clinical case manager stating that Mr. Chu had managed Philip's care for many years and that during a period that he did not manage the care Philip's health declined until Mr. Chu resumed. Ms. Dolch reported that Mr. Chu has been Philip's case manager for many years, that he is very involved with Philip, that he shops for Philip and takes him on outings, that the expenditures typically get approved in advance unless there is something planned last minute then Mr. Chu pays and submits a request for reimbursement and that Philip's quality of life is due to Mr. Chu's attention. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
